scan.title: Skin Image Analysis
scan.subtitle: Upload an image or use your live webcam for instant AI skin analysis.
scan.cameraHint: Please allow camera access in your browser settings and try again.
scan.tip: Position the skin area inside the blue frame. Ensure good lighting for better accuracy.

diseaseInfo.title: Disease Awareness
diseaseInfo.subtitle: Learn about the 10 skin conditions detected by DermaLens. Click any card to see full details.
diseaseInfo.highWarning: High risk — consult a dermatologist immediately if you notice these symptoms.
diseaseInfo.mediumWarning: Medium risk — monitor regularly and see a doctor if symptoms worsen.
diseaseInfo.disclaimer: Images shown are medical reference illustrations. Always consult a certified dermatologist for proper diagnosis.

acne.name: Acne
acne.description: A common skin condition where hair follicles become clogged with oil and dead skin cells, causing pimples, blackheads and whiteheads.
acne.visualDesc: Inflamed red bumps or whiteheads commonly on face, chest and back

actinicKeratosis.name: Actinic Keratosis
actinicKeratosis.description: Rough, scaly patch caused by years of sun exposure. Considered pre-cancerous and should be monitored.
actinicKeratosis.visualDesc: Dry, rough, scaly patch of skin — typically red, pink or brown

basalCell.name: Basal Cell Carcinoma
basalCell.description: Most common skin cancer. Rarely spreads but needs treatment. Often looks like a pearly bump.
basalCell.visualDesc: Shiny, translucent bump with a pearly or waxy appearance

eczema.name: Eczema
eczema.description: A chronic inflammatory skin condition causing itchy, red and cracked skin. Often linked to allergies or asthma.
eczema.visualDesc: Dry, scaly and inflamed skin — commonly on hands, elbows and behind knees

psoriasis.name: Psoriasis
psoriasis.description: A chronic autoimmune condition causing rapid skin cell buildup, resulting in scaling and inflammation.
psoriasis.visualDesc: Red patches covered with thick silver or white scaly skin

ringworm.name: Ringworm
ringworm.description: A fungal infection of the skin causing a ring-shaped red rash. Despite the name, it is not caused by a worm.
ringworm.visualDesc: Circular red ring with clearer skin in the middle — itchy and scaly

rosacea.name: Rosacea
rosacea.description: A chronic skin condition causing redness and visible blood vessels in the face, sometimes with acne-like bumps.
rosacea.visualDesc: Persistent redness across nose and cheeks — may have small pus-filled bumps

seborrheicKeratosis.name: Seborrheic Keratosis
seborrheicKeratosis.description: Common non-cancerous skin growths that appear with age. They look waxy or stuck-on and vary in color.
seborrheicKeratosis.visualDesc: Rough, waxy or scaly surface — looks like it is stuck on to the skin

vitiligo.name: Vitiligo
vitiligo.description: A condition where skin loses its pigment cells, resulting in white patches on the skin.
vitiligo.visualDesc: Smooth white patches on skin — clearly defined edges where pigment is lost
lookFor: Hair in patch may be white

warts.name: Warts
warts.description: Small rough growths on the skin caused by human papillomavirus (HPV). Common on hands and feet.
warts.visualDesc: Small rough bumps with a cauliflower-like texture — skin-colored or darker

precautions.title: Precautions & Skin Care
precautions.subtitle: Follow these guidelines to maintain healthy skin and prevent skin diseases.
precautions.warningBody: If you notice any rapidly changing skin lesion, immediately consult a certified dermatologist. Early detection saves lives.

checks.title: Regular Skin Checks
tips: Look for new or changing moles
Use the ABCDE rule for moles

hydration.title: Skin Hydration
tips: Drink at least 8 glasses of water
Avoid hot showers — use warm water

doctor.title: When to See a Doctor
tips: Mole changes in size or color
Any skin change that worries you
